Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Undroidwish – a single-file, batteries-included Tcl/Tk binary for many platforms 54 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. rpg.actor Game Jam 59 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 322 upvotes, 53 comments
  4. Circuit-level PDP-11/34 emulator 43 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Paper Tape Is All You Need – Training a Transformer on a 1976 Minicomputer 131 upvotes, 21 comments
  6. Detecting file changes on macOS with kqueue 56 upvotes, 7 comments
  7. Linux is an interpreter 146 upvotes, 27 comments
  8. Meta Partners with Arm to Develop New Class of Data Center Silicon 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 591 upvotes, 142 comments
  10. My heuristics are wrong. What now? 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Spanish legislation as a Git repo 681 upvotes, 213 comments
  12. The bee that everyone wants to save 269 upvotes, 83 comments
  13. OpenCiv1 – open-source rewrite of Civ1 59 upvotes, 13 comments
  14. Cocoa-Way – Native macOS Wayland compositor for running Linux apps seamlessly 293 upvotes, 93 comments
  15. I decompiled the White House's new app 335 upvotes, 117 comments
  16. CSS is DOOMed 121 upvotes, 37 comments
  17. Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting 111 upvotes, 34 comments
  18. The first 40 months of the AI era 80 upvotes, 24 comments
  19. CERN uses ultra-compact AI models on FPGAs for real-time LHC data filtering 296 upvotes, 130 comments
  20. Private equity turned vulnerable elderly people into human ATMs 48 upvotes, 15 comments
  21. AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition crams 208MB of cache into a single chip 292 upvotes, 162 comments
  22. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 125 upvotes, 84 comments
  23. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 489 upvotes, 379 comments
  24. Goldman Sachs now reckons that oil could take out the 2008 record of $147 13 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. Hacking old hardware by renaming to .zip [video] 95 upvotes, 72 comments
  26. C++26: A User-Friednly assert() macro 59 upvotes, 45 comments
  27. Google just gave Android power users a sideloading win 43 upvotes, 37 comments
  28. 1929: Inside the Greatest Crash in Wall Street History 33 upvotes, 28 comments
  29. ICAO issued new power bank restriction on flight 52 upvotes, 70 comments
  30. InpharmD (YC W21) Is Hiring – Senior Ruby on Rails Developer 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 7:30am, 29 March 2026. Source here.
